name: Expired PMID (the record the cleanup sweep purges)
description: The lifecycle counterpart to 01: a loyalty PMID that has passed its end_date. This is exactly the shape Trigger 2 (the native B2C cron sweep) and Trigger 1 (webhook) select for deletion, enabled:false, past end_date, c_loyaltyStatus EXPIRED, still flagged c_isLoyaltyManaged + c_loyaltyAutoCleanup. Use it to build/test the housekeeping path (section 5).
notes: Cron-sweep selection predicate (Trigger 2): c_isLoyaltyManaged==true AND c_loyaltyAutoCleanup==true AND end_date older than N days. Once selected, the script DELETEs the PMID and its CG-<code> group. B2C already disabled it natively at end_date (enabled:false) so no accidental discount occurred in the gap. A c_loyaltyStatus:'ARCHIVED' variant (keep the record but exclude from purge) is the same shape with c_loyaltyAutoCleanup:false.
